div.head: Mr. Adams to Mr. Seward.

opener.dateline: Legation of the United States, London, January 2, 1867.

div.p: Sir: At the particular request of Mr. Morse, I transmit herewith a statement made by him in answer to the extraordinary deposition of Mr. Hull, constituting a part of the proceedings in the suit with Mr. Prioleau. The more obvious and proper course would seem to have been to prepare a deposition to be used, if necessary, in a later stage of the case itself. I have taken the liberty to suggest this to Mr. Morse, in order that his statement may be as fully accessible on the record as Mr. Hull’s, to which it replies.
I have the honor to be, sir, your obedient servant,

postscript.p: Hon. William H. Seward, Secretary of State, Washington, D. C.
